In addition, section 1106(d) inserted the following condition: “subject to the condition that $50,000,000 of that amount shall be used to carry out section 206 of the Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2015 ( note; )”. The amendment by section 1106(d) could not be executed as written because “$530,000,000” no longer appeared in text after the amendment by section 203, and therefore, the text does not reflect the insertion of the condition. , subsequently amended subsec. children.heading: Authorization of grants and cooperative agreements
children.label: a Authorization of grants and cooperative agreements
A
children.text: to conserve water;

children.label: B
children.text: to increase water use efficiency;

children.label: C
children.text: to facilitate water markets;

children.label: D
children.text: to enhance water management, including increasing the use of renewable energy in the management and delivery of water;

children.label: E
children.text: to accelerate the adoption and use of advanced water treatment technologies to increase water supply;

children.label: F
children.text: to assist States and water users in complying with interstate compacts or reducing basin water supply-demand imbalances;

children.label: G
children.text: to achieve the prevention of the decline of species that the United States Fish and Wildlife Service and National Marine Fisheries Service have proposed for listing under the Endangered Species Act of 1973 ( et seq.) (or candidate species that are being considered by those agencies for such listing but are not yet the subject of a proposed rule);16 U.S.C. 1531

children.label: H
children.text: to achieve the acceleration of the recovery of threatened species, endangered species, and designated critical habitats that are adversely affected by Federal reclamation projects or are subject to a recovery plan or conservation plan under the Endangered Species Act of 1973 ( et seq.) under which the Commissioner of Reclamation has implementation responsibilities;16 U.S.C. 1531

children.label: I
children.text: to improve the condition of a natural feature; or

children.label: i
children.text: to address any climate-related impact to the water supply of the United States that increases ecological resiliency to the impacts of climate change;

children.label: ii
children.text: to prevent any water-related crisis or conflict at any watershed that has a nexus to a Federal reclamation project located in a service area; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to plan for or address the impacts of drought.

children.label: i
children.text: the States and areas referred to in ;section 391 of title 43

children.label: ii
children.text: the State of Alaska;

children.label: iii
children.text: the State of Hawaii; or

children.label: iv
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: i
children.text: a proposal of the improvement or activity to be planned, designed, constructed, or implemented by the eligible applicant; and

children.label: I
children.text: a proposal for a monitoring plan of at least 5 years that would demonstrate ways in which the proposed improvement or activity would result in improved streamflows or aquatic habitat; or

children.label: II
children.text: for a project that does not anticipate improved streamflows or aquatic habitat, an analysis of ways in which the proposed improvement or activity would contribute to 1 or more of the other objectives described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Requirements of grants and cooperative agreements
children.label: 3 Requirements of grants and cooperative agreements

children.heading: Compliance with requirements
children.label: A Compliance with requirements
children.text: Each grant and agreement entered into by the Secretary with any eligible applicant under paragraph (1) shall be in compliance with each requirement described in subparagraphs (B) through (F).

children.label: I
children.text: to use any associated water savings to increase the total irrigated acreage of the eligible applicant; or

children.label: II
children.text: to otherwise increase the consumptive use of water in the operation of the eligible applicant, as determined pursuant to the law of the State in which the operation of the eligible applicant is located.

children.label: aa
children.text: a court decree;

children.label: bb
children.text: a settlement;

children.label: cc
children.text: a law; or

children.label: dd
children.text: any combination of the authorities described in items (aa) through (cc); or

children.label: II
children.text: to otherwise increase the consumptive use of water more than the water right of the Indian tribe described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Nonreimbursable funds
children.label: C Nonreimbursable funds
children.text: Any funds provided by the Secretary to an eligible applicant through a grant or agreement under paragraph (1) shall be nonreimbursable.

children.heading: Title to improvements
children.label: D Title to improvements
children.text: If an infrastructure improvement to a federally owned facility is the subject of a grant or other agreement entered into between the Secretary and an eligible applicant under paragraph (1), the Federal Government shall continue to hold title to the facility and improvements to the facility.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II), the Federal share of the cost of any infrastructure improvement or activity that is the subject of a grant or other agreement entered into between the Secretary and an eligible applicant under paragraph (1) shall not exceed 50 percent of the cost of the infrastructure improvement or activity.

children.heading: Increased Federal share for certain infrastructure improvements and activities
children.label: II Increased Federal share for certain infrastructure improvements and activities
AA
children.text: a watershed group (as defined in ); orsection 1015 of title 16

children.label: BB
children.text: a water user and 1 or more stakeholders with diverse interests; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the majority of the benefits of the infrastructure improvement or activity, as determined by the Secretary, are for the purpose of advancing 1 or more components of an established strategy or plan to increase the reliability of water supply for consumptive and nonconsumptive ecological values.

children.label: I
children.text: consider the value of any in-kind services that substantially contributes toward the completion of the improvement or activity, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: II
children.text: not consider any other amount that the eligible applicant receives from a Federal agency.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: iii Maximum amount
children.text: The amount provided to an eligible applicant through a grant or other agreement under paragraph (1) shall be not more than $5,000,000.

children.heading: Operation and maintenance costs
children.label: iv Operation and maintenance costs
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of operating and maintaining any infrastructure improvement that is the subject of a grant or other agreement entered into between the Secretary and an eligible applicant under paragraph (1) shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided under chapter 171 of title 28 (commonly known as the “Federal Tort Claims Act”), the United States shall not be liable for monetary damages of any kind for any injury arising out of an act, omission, or occurrence that arises in relation to any facility created or improved under this section, the title of which is not held by the United States.

children.heading: Tort Claims Act
children.label: ii Tort Claims Act
children.text: Nothing in this section increases the liability of the United States beyond that provided in chapter 171 of title 28 (commonly known as the “Federal Tort Claims Act”).

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 4 Priority
children.text: In providing grants to, and entering into agreements for, projects intended to have a quantifiable water savings under this subsection, the Secretary shall give priority to projects that enhance drought resilience by benefitting the water supply and ecosystem.

children.label: A
children.text: to conserve water resources;

children.label: B
children.text: to increase the efficiency of the use of water resources;

children.label: C
children.text: to restore a natural feature or use a nature-based feature to reduce water supply and demand imbalances or the risk of drought or flood; or

children.label: D
children.text: to enhance the management of water resources, including increasing the use of renewable energy in the management and delivery of water.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An agreement entered into between the Secretary and any university, institution, or organization described in paragraph (1) shall be subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: B Availability
children.text: The agreements under this subsection shall be available to all Reclamation projects and programs that may benefit from project-specific or programmatic cooperative research and development.

children.heading: Mutual benefit
children.label: c Mutual benefit
children.text: Grants or other agreements made under this section may be for the mutual benefit of the United States and the entity that is provided the grant or enters into the cooperative agreement.

children.heading: Relationship to project-specific authority
children.label: d Relationship to project-specific authority
children.text: This section shall not supersede any existing project-specific funding authority.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $1,000,000,000, to remain available until expended.
